frameworks.name: ensemble / summary-statistic representation
frameworks.one_line: Visual system encodes high-order summary statistics alongside noisy sensory inputs hierarchically.

frameworks.name: population-level neural decoding (EEG IEM-style)
frameworks.one_line: Multivariate decoding of remembered features from human EEG.

frameworks.name: dynamic vs stable working-memory coding
frameworks.one_line: Contrasts dynamically-evolving and time-stable population codes.

core_question: How does the human brain represent ensemble summary statistics versus individual features in visual working memory?
key_assumptions: Ensemble mean is a structured WM representation distinct from item features
EEG topography indexes distinct cortical sources (frontocentral vs occipitoparietal)
Task demands shape posterior visual coding
manipulations: Task: old/new judgment vs continuous estimation
Representation type: individual feature vs ensemble mean
key_findings: Frontocentral electrodes show stable coding of features and ensemble mean
Frontocentral ensemble-mean coding correlates with behavioral ensemble indices across individuals
Occipitoparietal sites code features dynamically; ensemble mean absent in old/new task
Continuous estimation task evokes both dynamic and stable codes in occipitoparietal sites
interpretations: Prefrontal cortex maintains behaviorally-relevant abstract WM representations
Posterior visual representations are modulated by task demands
